mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 1.15 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises fast enough to signal persistent inflation, investors price tighter future policy and lower real earnings quality, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 244.048 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-inflation observations do not have lower mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS indicates improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 478873.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes generate a walk-forward 5-day directional hit rate at or below 50% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and recession risk, reducing expected corporate revenues and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 4.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or have nonnegative average excess return.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is flat or falling, payroll income growth supports consumption without immediate labor-market stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 146615.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-flat-or-down condition has a 20-day forward return mean no higher than the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Falling HOUST combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals that higher financing costs are suppressing interest-sensitive demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month percent change is below -10% and FEDFUNDS 6-month change is above 0
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 1215.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the estimated interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in INDPRO signals weakening manufacturing output and margin pressure from lower capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 105.16 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive year-over-year trend in GDPC1 signals broad real demand growth and improving aggregate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year percent change is above 0 and RSAFS 3-month percent change is above 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 17010.7 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS demand signal fails to exceed the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return mean in at least 60% of walk-forward folds.
